steps.title: Finding $a$ by matching the $x^3$ coefficient
steps.content:
When we expand $(ax + 3)(5x^2 - bx + 4)$, the $x^3$ term comes from $ax \cdot 5x^2 = 5ax^3$.

The right side has $20x^3$.

Therefore: $$5a = 20 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a = 4$$

steps.title: Finding $ab$ by matching the $x^2$ coefficient
steps.content:
When we expand, the $x^2$ terms come from:
  • $ax\cdot (-bx) = -abx^2$
  • $3\cdot 5x^2 = 15x^2$
Combined, the $x^2$ coefficient is $-ab+15$. The right side has coefficient $-9$, so:
$$-ab+15=-9$$
$$ab=24$$
This matches, so C is correct.
